review: At first, when my wife and I were introduced to Kelsey Garner from the Ben Kinney Team, we were excited to work with this company. She sat us down in her office and explained the process for purchasing a home and made us feel comfortable as first time home buyers. We later found a house that we fell in love with and decided to make an offer. She recommended that we offer higher than the asking price, so we did. She recommended we offer $3500-$4000, or 1%, as Ernest money, so we did. She then recommended we work with BKCO Mortgage and get a home loan through them, so we did. We listened to her advice and got burned. The original estimates for the home were a little over 1900 but under $2000 including HOA. As soon as we started signing paperwork it turns out the HOA was never included. Coleman Paige of BKCO said that these numbers are "estimates" and the monthly payments will go down as we get more information post-appraisal. The numbers never went down. My wife and I were looking at over $2100 a month, which we could not pay comfortably. After paying for the inspection and cutting a check for $4000 of earnest money, we told Kelsey, prior to the appraisal, "we want out, and we want the earnest money back." She recommended we wait for the appraisal because that's the last contingency to back out of the purchase and get our money back. But that was bologna. We listened to her advice and when the appraisal came in, the house was appraised to the exact dollar amount we offered. She then said that it'll be "complicated" to get our money back. My wife and I wound up losing $5200 in total for NOT buying the home and we felt as if we were being forced to buy or else. We took the hit instead. She even told me that if I had not completed the appraisal then I would have gotten my money back, so why did she offer that advice? In sum, find a different company, find a different agent, and DO NOT USE BKCO mortgage. It was nice at the beginning but if you try back out, they'll take you for what they can.
